creator: smh@FreeBSD.org
text:
TBH I don't know of anyone who uses copies as its only one part of the bigger resiliency picture.

It's much more common to use mirror or RAIDZ* which gives you both forms of protection and I believe those are part of the installer already. I say believe as we use mfsbsd for all our installs: http://mfsbsd.vx.sk/

Don't forget that in using UFS there not only do you not get bit-rot protection you also don't get detection of it which you get with ZFS no matter how you configure it.

We've been using ZFS in FreeBSD since its integration and I can honestly say it makes the operation and maintenance so much easier we would literally never go back.
